text: Handling deadlines effectively is a crucial skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management involves breaking a large project into smaller tasks and setting milestone dates for each. Prioritization means deciding which tasks are most urgent or important, focusing your energy there first. Scope management is equally vital; it prevents you from adding extra features or work beyond the original plan. Finally, clear communication with colleagues or supervisors about progress and potential delays can prevent last-minute crises. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and more consistent results.
